titleInfo.title: Supplementary Corrigenda to the Epigraphica of Eight Gems Studio

mods.abstract: The present form of the well-known Supplementary Corrigenda to the Epigraphica of Eight Gems Studio (Baqiongshi jinshi buzheng 八瓊室金石補正), was only compiled in 1925, but its information dates from the life time of its author, Lu Zengxiang 陸增祥 (1833-1889). Lu Zengxiang begins his entry on Mount Gang with the rubbings of the inscriptions on Chicken Beak Rock. The names of the signatories with the date (E) as well as the three Buddha names (D1) he transcribes in full. Of the two sections of the sutra C1 and C2 he quotes the beginning and the end, but, as usual, he does not identify the text. From the Lankāvatara sutra version A he quotes two sections and from version B nine sections. He notes that Ruan Yuan records none of these eleven sections, but that the rubbings, which he consults, must come from Mount Gang, because the labels on them say so. Only a future visit can bring certainty, he notes. (LL)

This work gives for every inscription entry the measurements, the amount of columns, the location, a transcription and a commentary concerning older literature. For the Gangshan entries Lu Zengxiang remarks that although his eleven entries are not found in the older literature, he assumes that they all belong to Mount Gang because the notes on the rubbings say so. He is aware of the fact that the inscriptions on Mount Jian and Mount Tie are connected to those of Mount Gang, as was stated in Shanzuo jinshi zhi. Certainty about the location of the inscriptions can only be brought about a future visit.
mods.tableOfContents: fascicles 12-16: 北魏, fascicles 17-19: 東魏, facsicles 20-22: 北齊, fascicle 23: 北周， facsicles 24-28: 隨, facsicles 29-78: 唐
